title: Memory-safe flying delighting translator + from-scratch trunk: the never-fairly-tested canonicalization pivot, corrected
hypothesis: 18 straight rounds (36-53) have failed to beat champion exp-35's 743.07 m. Splitting them by what they actually changed gives a clean signal: every one of the 11 rounds that swapped or reparameterized the DECISION mechanism while the descriptor-producing trunk was either untouched or forcibly swapped (36-40, 45, 46, 48-53 -- margin losses, coordinate-generated fields, coarse-to-fine, retrieve-rerank, factorized axes, dispatcher+MoE, per-patch consensus, residual VQ, Gaussian-mixture regression, a ShuffleNetV2 trunk paired with FiLM+top-K decode) landed clearly worse than champion, several catastrophically (1e9 x5, 1552-2752 m x6). The ONE round that instead kept the champion's exact field+soft-argmax decode shape and only changed what the trunk was trained to represent (exp 42, domain-native contrastive from-scratch pretraining) came closest of all 17 post-champion rounds at 846.42 m (+14%) -- the tightest gap in the entire losing streak, and the only result inside 1000 m. That single data point argues descriptor/appearance-canonicalization quality, not decision-mechanism shape, is the real remaining lever: champion's ImageNet-MobileNetV3 trunk (now banned) was never taught to discard lighting as a nuisance variable, and no downstream decode rewrite has ever fixed that by construction. CLAUDE.md SS4 explicitly names learned relighting as one of the areas most open to genuine improvement, and library L1 proposes exactly this ('canonicalize the night: denoise toward the clean render') -- but this exact family was tried twice (exp 43 joint reconstruction, exp 44 sequential flying translator) and BOTH were never actually measured: their train logs show a clean 0-byte crash on 2 of 4 areas each round (different area-pairs each time, ruling out an area-specific cause) diagnosed as an OOM/ENOSPC race from loop.sh's 4-way concurrent GPU launch colliding with a full-resolution (128x128) encoder-decoder's large intermediate activations. That is an infra artifact, not a refutation -- the canonicalization bet itself remains untested at the design level, exactly like exp 47/48's per-patch-consensus idea was before its own corrected re-attempt (exp 49) finally measured it (and refuted it, cleanly, this time). This design is that same kind of corrected re-attempt, not a repeat of 43 or 44: the translator is redesigned from scratch to make the OOM/ENOSPC failure structurally impossible (bottlenecked at 32x32 with narrow channels, one pass per crop instead of one pass per C4-rotation-copy) rather than hoping the same shape gets luckier concurrency timing, and the reconstruction loss is computed at a downsampled 32x32 target instead of full 128x128 pixels -- directly following exp 44's own pre-registered falsification fork (c), which predicted that a full-resolution L1 term risks oversmoothing genuine layout detail (building edges) while chasing pixel fidelity, and recommended exactly this fix ('weight the L1 term lower or restrict it to a coarser scale') for the next attempt.
method: Insert a tiny from-scratch delighting translator ahead of the trunk in model/model.py: Conv2d(3,16,k=4,s=4) -> 32x32x16, two 3x3 GroupNorm+ReLU convs at 32x32x24, Conv2d(24,3,k=3) -> a 32x32x3 raw correction. The correction is bilinearly upsampled (parameter-free) to 128x128 and added residually to the input crop (clamped to [0,1]) to produce the 'cleaned' image the rest of the network sees; peak activation size never exceeds 32x32x24, and the crop is translated ONCE (not once per C4 rotation copy, unlike exp 44's ordering) -- both changes directly shrink the per-example memory footprint that the OOM/ENOSPC crashes were traced to. Replace the ImageNet-pretrained MobileNetV3-Small trunk (banned this round) with a from-scratch 4-stage plain conv stack (32/64/96/128 channels, stride 2 each, GroupNorm+ReLU), 128x128 -> 8x8x128, reading the CLEANED image's four C4 rotations. Delete the dual-expert lighting-gate blend (loc_logits/dark_logits/gate) entirely -- one unified field (GAP + 1x1-squeezed layout code -> GRID_K x GRID_K logits), matching the only structural choice (42/43/44) that removed the gate without itself causing a measured regression. Keep the existing sharpened-softmax soft-argmax decode exactly (DECODE_BETA mechanism unchanged in form; it is 'changed' only in that it now reads a single field instead of a gated blend, forced by the gate's removal). Confidence head gains one new detached input: the translator's own mean |correction| magnitude (how much repair the crop needed), alongside the existing peak/entropy/sharpened-vs-unsharpened-decode-gap features. Training: sample_epoch also extracts the pixel-aligned clean daytime reference crop (same cx/cy/angle, from reference.tif, already loaded for compute_cell_weights) for every drawn training location -- a free supervision pair per library L1. loss_fn gains a reconstruction term: L1 between (avg_pool2d(input,4) + raw_correction).clamp(0,1) and avg_pool2d(reference_crop,4), weight 0.3 (down from exp 43/44's implicit 1.0, per the falsification-fork-(c) fix). Training schedule: single LR group for all parameters (translator + trunk + head), since nothing is pretrained -- the old 10x trunk/head split existed only to protect transferred ImageNet features, which no longer exist.
expected_outcome: If descriptor/appearance-canonicalization quality is really the dominant lever the last 18 rounds jointly point at (as exp 42's 846.42 m result, the closest of the entire losing streak, suggests), and a fair, uncrashed measurement of the reconstruction family confirms it, worst-case median error should land materially below exp 42's 846.42 m -- targeting roughly 550-800 m (0-26% better than champion), since an explicit pixel-aligned canonicalization signal is a more direct fix for lighting nuisance than contrastive instance-discrimination, which only pushes different-location embeddings apart in the abstract with no pressure to preserve what a location actually looks like. Mechanism-level signature to check post-hoc: the reconstruction L1 loss should converge to a visibly low value (<0.08 on [0,1] pixels, at 32x32 resolution) by the end of training, and both berlin/prignitz/munich/frankfurt train logs should show clean per-epoch loss lines with no 0-byte crash (confirming the OOM/ENOSPC fix actually worked this time). Falsification forks: (a) if training is clean (no crash, healthy recon loss <0.08) but the primary metric does not beat 846.42 m, the reconstruction/canonicalization family is now genuinely refuted at the design level for the first time -- distinct from exp 43/44's uninformative crashes -- and future rounds should redirect firmly away from lighting-invariance objectives (contrastive AND reconstruction both tried, both measured, neither beating a plain trunk swap) toward raw trunk capacity or training-data scale instead; (b) if recon loss stays high/flat (>0.15), the 32x32-bottleneck translator is too constrained for even a downsampled target, arguing for a slightly wider mid-channel count next round rather than abandoning the flying-translator idea; (c) if the metric regresses sharply despite healthy recon loss and clean logs, translating once-then-rotating (instead of exp 44's rotate-then-translate) likely cost real information -- the correction may need to be heading-aware, which the next iteration should test directly; (d) if any area's log is still 0 bytes, the OOM/ENOSPC fix did not fully work and the result must be read as another uninformative infra artifact, not a refutation.
eli5: Two earlier attempts tried teaching the model to first 'clean up' a dark, noisy night photo into something that looks like a clear daytime picture before trying to figure out where it was taken -- like adjusting brightness and contrast before reading a blurry street sign. Both attempts crashed partway through training for a boring technical reason (the computer ran out of memory), so we never actually found out if the idea works. This round redesigns that cleanup step to be much smaller and lighter -- it works on a shrunk-down, thumbnail version of the picture instead of the full-size one -- so it can't run out of memory the same way, and we finally get to test the idea for real. Everything downstream is also rebuilt to match: a fresh, untrained map-reading network (since the old borrowed one is off-limits this round), with the old 'two separate experts, one for day, one for night' trick removed entirely, because the cleanup step should make that split unnecessary.

stages.name: Camera frame
stages.detail: 128x128 px aerial crop, one of 6 simulated lighting conditions

stages.name: Delighting translator
stages.detail: a tiny from-scratch filter shrinks the crop down to a 32x32 thumbnail internally, works out a correction there, then stretches that correction back up and adds it to the original photo -- repainting it to look closer to a clear daytime shot regardless of the input lighting. Runs once per crop, before anything else, and flies on the drone too

stages.name: Rotation fan
stages.detail: the CLEANED-UP photo (not the raw one) is copied into its four 90-degree turns, which all travel through the trunk together -- the cleanup step itself only has to run once per crop instead of four times, since repainting the lighting doesn't depend on which way the photo is turned

stages.name: Feature extractor
stages.detail: from-scratch 4-stage plain conv stack (32->64->96->128 channels, stride 2 each), 128x128 -> 8x8x128, no borrowed ImageNet weights, no MobileNetV3 family, no checkpoint file

stages.name: Descriptor
stages.detail: the trunk's overall texture average (GAP) plus a 1x1-squeezed spatial layout code, resized for the new 128-channel trunk

stages.name: Probability map
stages.detail: the old day-expert/night-expert blend is removed entirely -- one shared scorer reads the descriptor into a single probability map over the map cells, since the cleanup step now does the lighting-handling job the two-expert blend used to do

stages.name: Decode
stages.detail: same sharpen-then-balance-point mechanism as before, now reading the single unified map instead of a blended two-expert one

stages.name: Confidence
stages.detail: reads the map's own shape (crisp hotspot vs. washed-out smear) as before, plus a new signal: how much repair the cleanup step had to make to this particular crop -- a heavily-corrected crop is a hint the model is on less familiar ground

stages.name: Training data
stages.detail: each drawn training crop now also loads its exact pixel-aligned daytime twin from the untouched reference imagery, free of charge from the frozen relight pipeline, as an answer key for the cleanup step's own training

stages.name: Training signal
stages.detail: the usual grading (reward probability near the true spot, grade the coordinate, grade the confidence guess) plus a new, lightly-weighted rule: the cleanup step's own repainted thumbnail should look like the real daytime thumbnail of that spot

stages.name: Training schedule
stages.detail: one shared learning-rate glide for the whole network -- nothing is borrowed/pretrained anymore, so there is no separate slow-trunk / fast-head split to protect transferred features

implementation_brief: Edit model/model.py and model/train.py only. FROZEN CONTRACTS TO PRESERVE: train.py's CLI (--area/--out-dir/--data-dir/--epochs/--max-crops-per-bucket/--seed, unchanged argparse surface and train_area() return shape) and model.py's ONNX export contract (export_onnx takes a 1x3x128x128 float32 [0,1] input via model.forward(x) with default args and returns a single [1,3] tensor [[u, v, conf]] -- the frozen scorer pipeline/score.py depends on both exactly as documented in model.py's module docstring). model/model.py: (1) delete the `from torchvision.models import mobilenet_v3_small` import, `PRETRAINED_TRUNK_PATH`, `IMAGENET_MEAN`, `IMAGENET_STD` constants, `_build_pretrained_trunk()`, and the `norm_mean`/`norm_std` buffers plus their use in forward() -- no pretrained checkpoint of any kind is loaded anywhere in this file. (2) Add `class DelightTranslator(nn.Module)`: `down = nn.Conv2d(3,16,4,stride=4)` + GroupNorm(4,16) + ReLU -> 32x32x16; two `nn.Conv2d(c,24,3,padding=1)` + GroupNorm(4,24) + ReLU blocks at 32x32 (first takes 16->24, second 24->24); `out_conv = nn.Conv2d(24,3,3,padding=1)` -> raw_correction, a 32x32x3 tensor, no final activation. forward(x) returns (cleaned, raw_correction) where `correction_up = F.interpolate(raw_correction, size=(128,128), mode='bilinear', align_corners=False)` and `cleaned = (x + correction_up).clamp(0,1)`. (3) Replace `_build_pretrained_trunk` usage in `TinyLocNet.__init__`/`forward` with a new from-scratch `self.trunk = nn.Sequential(...)`: four blocks of Conv2d(in,out,3,stride=2,padding=1) + GroupNorm(8,out) + ReLU with channels 3->32->64->96->128 (128x128 -> 8x8x128); keep the existing `layout_squeeze = nn.Conv2d(feat_ch, layout_ch, 1)` and `loc_logits = nn.Linear(feat_ch + layout_ch*fmap_hw*fmap_hw, grid_k*grid_k)` pattern (feat_ch=128 now), but DELETE `dark_logits` and `gate` entirely -- `logits4 = self.loc_logits(torch.cat([f4, layout4], dim=1))` directly, no blend. (4) In `TinyLocNet.forward`, call `cleaned, raw_correction = self.translator(x)` ONCE on the raw [N,3,128,128] input before `_c4_stack`; feed `_c4_stack(cleaned)` to `self.trunk` (not the raw input). Keep the C4-average-then-sharpen decode exactly as today (same DECODE_BETA softmax/soft-argmax math, same `cell_u`/`cell_v` buffers, same `u1`/`v1`/`peak`/`ent`/`gap` confidence features). Add `recon_mag = raw_correction.abs().mean(dim=(1,2,3), keepdim=True)` (shape [N,1], computed pre-C4-stack so it is naturally per-example not per-rotation) and include it in `conf_head`'s input (`nn.Linear(feat_ch + 4, 32)` now, cat order: f.detach(), peak, ent, gap, recon_mag). When `return_logits=True`, additionally compute and return `recon_pred32 = (F.avg_pool2d(x, 4) + raw_correction).clamp(0,1)` as a third return value: `return out, logits, recon_pred32`. Default `return_logits=False` path is unaffected (still returns just `out`) -- export_onnx must keep working with zero code changes. (5) `loss_fn` gains two new required params, `recon_pred32` and `recon_target32`: add `recon_loss = nn.functional.l1_loss(recon_pred32, recon_target32)` and return `ce + coord_loss + 0.3 * conf_loss + 0.3 * recon_loss`. (6) Rewrite the module docstring: delete the Exp 11/12 paragraphs describing the pretrained trunk and the dual-expert gate (both mechanisms no longer exist in this file) and add one new paragraph describing this experiment's translator + single-field design and why (memory-safe vs. exp 43/44, downsampled reconstruction target per exp 44's own falsification fork). model/train.py: (1) in `train_area`, after `meta = load_meta(...)`, load `reference.tif` once via rasterio the same way `compute_cell_weights` does (`with rasterio.open(area_dir(area, data_dir) / 'reference.tif') as src: ref_img = src.read().transpose(1,2,0)`) and pass `ref_img` into `sample_epoch`. (2) In `sample_epoch`, for every drawn crop also call `extract_crop(ref_img, c['cx'], c['cy'], angle)` (same `angle` already used for the lit crop, so the pair stays pixel-aligned) and stack into a parallel `xs_ref` list; return `(x, x_ref, y)` where `x_ref = torch.from_numpy(np.stack(xs_ref))` (uint8, same convention as `x`). (3) In `train_area`'s inner batch loop, index `x_ref[idx]` with the same `perm` as `x`/`y`, convert to float [0,1] the same way (`.permute(0,3,1,2).contiguous().float().div_(255.0)`), compute `recon_target32 = F.avg_pool2d(xb_ref, 4)`, call `out, logits, recon_pred32 = model(xb, return_logits=True)`, and pass `recon_pred32, recon_target32` into `loss_fn`. (4) Replace the two-group optimizer (`trunk_params` at 1e-4 / `head_params` at 1e-3) with a single `torch.optim.Adam(model.parameters(), lr=1e-3)` group -- nothing is pretrained, so there is no transferred-feature protection rationale left. (5) Update the `info` dict's `"init"` field to `"from-scratch: delighting translator (32x32 bottleneck, residual correction) + 4-stage conv trunk, no pretrained weights anywhere"`. Leave `compute_cell_weights`, `prepare_realizations`, `calibrate_conf_shift`, `GRID_K`, `TARGET_SIGMA_CELLS`, `DECODE_BETA`, `GOOD_ERR_UV`, the confusability-weighted sampler, and everything in pipeline/ untouched.
